controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate access control policy and procedures.

controls.title: Account Management
controls.description: Manage information system accounts including establishing, activating, modifying, reviewing, disabling, and removing accounts.

controls.title: Access Enforcement
controls.description: Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources.

controls.title: Least Privilege
controls.description: Employ the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses necessary for users to accomplish assigned tasks.

controls.title: Remote Access
controls.description: Establish and document usage restrictions, configuration/connection requirements, and implementation guidance for remote access.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate identification and authentication policy and procedures.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
controls.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate organizational users and the processes acting on behalf of organizational users.

controls.title: Authenticator Management
controls.description: Manage information system authenticators by verifying identity of individual, group, role, or device receiving authenticator.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Non-Organizational Users)
controls.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate non-organizational users.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate system and information integrity policy and procedures.

controls.title: Flaw Remediation
controls.description: Identify, report, and correct information system flaws; test software and firmware updates before installation.

controls.title: Malicious Code Protection
controls.description: Implement malicious code protection mechanisms at information system entry and exit points to detect and eradicate malicious code.

controls.title: System Monitoring
controls.description: Monitor the system to detect attacks, indicators of potential attacks, and unauthorized local, network, and remote connections.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate audit and accountability policy and procedures.

controls.title: Event Logging
controls.description: Identify the types of events that the system is capable of logging in support of the audit function.
controls.fedramp_note: Coordinate the event logging function with other organizations requiring audit-related information.

controls.title: Content of Audit Records
controls.description: Ensure that audit records contain sufficient information to establish what events occurred, the sources of the events, and the outcomes of the events.

controls.title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
controls.description: Review and analyze system audit records at least weekly for indications of inappropriate or unusual activity.

controls.title: Audit Record Generation
controls.description: Provide audit record generation capability for the event types defined in AU-2; allow designated personnel to select which auditable events are to be logged.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate configuration management policy and procedures.

controls.title: Baseline Configuration
controls.description: Develop, document, and maintain under configuration control, a current baseline configuration of the information system.

controls.title: Configuration Settings
controls.description: Establish and document configuration settings for information technology products employed within the information system that reflect the most restrictive mode consistent with operational requirements.
controls.fedramp_note: USGCB and DISA STIGs must be used as configuration baselines where available.

controls.title: Least Functionality
controls.description: Configure the information system to provide only essential capabilities, prohibiting or restricting the use of functions, ports, protocols, and services not required.
